county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Webster County formed 1867 from unorganized territory. County seat is Red Cloud. Willa Cather's childhood home. Republican River valley. My Antonia and O Pioneers! settings.
county_metadata.research_tips: Webster County (formed 1867, seat Red Cloud) records are at the courthouse in Red Cloud. Red Cloud is famous as the childhood home of Willa Cather, whose novels (My Antonia, O Pioneers!) depict Nebraska pioneer life. The Willa Cather Foundation in Red Cloud preserves Cather-related sites and research materials. The Cather archives document many real families and places fictionalized in the novels. Homestead entries at BLM GLO document original settlers. The Republican River valley runs through the county. German, Scandinavian, and Anglo-American settlers predominated. FamilySearch has microfilmed county records. The 1885 state census is available. History Nebraska holds supplementary materials. Red Cloud newspapers from the 1870s-1880s are valuable sources.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO patents for original federal land entries in Webster County. County Register of Deeds in Red Cloud holds subsequent land transfers. Many counties do not have online access to county-level land records.

source_profiles.source_name: Webster County Deed Records -- Register of Deeds
source_profiles.repository: Webster County Register of Deeds, Red Cloud
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1867 to present. Nebraska is a public land state; initial federal patents are at BLM GLO (glorecords.blm.gov). Subsequent transfers recorded at the county Register of Deeds. Includes deeds, mortgages, liens, and plats.

source_profiles.source_name: Webster County Marriage Records -- County Court
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage records from 1867. Nebraska marriage licenses issued by county judges (County Court). State registration began 1909. Earlier marriages recorded at county level. FamilySearch has indexed many Nebraska marriages.

source_profiles.source_name: Webster County Probate and Estate Records -- County Court
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Wills, administrations, guardianships, and estate inventories from 1867. Nebraska probate is handled by the County Court. Records include wills, letters of administration, estate inventories, guardianship files, and adoption records.

source_profiles.source_name: Webster County Federal and State Census Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal census records for Webster County (1870-1950). The 1890 census was destroyed by fire. Nebraska conducted a state census in 1885 (available on microfilm). Territorial censuses (1854-1856, 1860) exist for early counties. Available through FamilySearch and Ancestry.
source_profiles.survival_notes: 1890 federal census destroyed nationally; Nebraska 1885 state census survives

source_profiles.source_name: Webster County Federal Land Patents -- BLM GLO
source_profiles.repository: Bureau of Land Management, General Land Office Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Original federal land patents for Webster County including homestead entries, pre-emption claims, timber culture entries, cash sales, and military warrant locations. Nebraska is a public land state -- all original land transfers from federal ownership are documented here. Searchable online by name and location.

source_profiles.source_name: Webster County Cemetery Records -- Find A Grave / Historical Society
source_profiles.repository: Find A Grave / Webster County Historical Society / Nebraska State Genealogical Society
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Cemetery transcriptions and burial records for Webster County. Find A Grave has growing coverage. Township, church, and family cemeteries are common in rural Nebraska. County historical societies and the Nebraska State Genealogical Society have published cemetery transcription volumes.

source_profiles.source_name: Webster County Records on Microfilm -- History Nebraska
source_profiles.coverage_notes: History Nebraska (NSHS) holds microfilm copies of Webster County records including deed indexes, probate records, marriage records, county board proceedings, and selected court records. Also holds manuscript collections, photographs, and maps related to Webster County history.

source_profiles.source_name: Webster County Historic Newspapers
source_profiles.repository: History Nebraska / Library of Congress / Newspapers.com
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Historic newspapers for Webster County communities. Obituaries, legal notices, marriage announcements, homestead proof notices, estate settlements, and community news. History Nebraska holds extensive microfilm of Nebraska newspapers. Chronicling America has digitized selections.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Wheeler County formed 1877 from unorganized territory. County seat is Bartlett. North-central Nebraska. Cedar River. Small population. Parent to Garfield County (1884).
county_metadata.research_tips: Wheeler County (formed 1877, seat Bartlett) in north-central Nebraska was carved from unorganized territory. Records are at the courthouse in Bartlett. This small county was settled by homesteaders in the 1880s. BLM GLO records document land entries. The Cedar River runs through the county. Anglo-American and German settlers predominated. FamilySearch has limited coverage. History Nebraska holds supplementary materials. Researchers should check neighboring Garfield and Greeley county records.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO patents for original federal land entries in Wheeler County. County Register of Deeds in Bartlett holds subsequent land transfers. Many counties do not have online access to county-level land records.

county_metadata.parent_county: Original county (Nebraska Territory, as Polk then York)
county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: York County formed 1855 as original Nebraska Territory county. County seat is York. Central Nebraska. York College (1890). Burlington Railroad junction. German and Swedish settlement.
county_metadata.research_tips: York County (formed 1855, seat York) is one of the original Nebraska Territory counties. Records are at the courthouse in York. York College (Christian Churches/Churches of Christ, 1890) is located here. The Anna Bemis Palmer Museum in York has local history collections. Homestead entries at BLM GLO document early settlers. German, Swedish, and Anglo-American settlers predominated. The Burlington and Missouri River Railroad brought many settlers. FamilySearch has microfilmed county records. The territorial and 1885 state censuses are available. History Nebraska holds supplementary materials.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO patents for original federal land entries in York County. County Register of Deeds in York holds subsequent land transfers. Many counties do not have online access to county-level land records.
